/api/config/skip-apps.rationale: The desktop app reads and patches skip-app settings through the stronger sparse /api/config contract.
/api/config/skip-apps.next_action: No app work unless the daemon removes the legacy route.

/api/context.rationale: Context bundles are trust-gated MCP/agent calls, not a desktop user command.
/api/context.next_action: Keep hidden unless a diagnostics context panel is designed.

/api/memory/entities/{entity_id}/observa.rationale: IdentityDetail already adds observations through /api/memory/observations with a typed entity_id request body.
/api/memory/entities/{entity_id}/observa.next_action: No direct wrapper unless the generic observation route is deprecated.

/api/memory/link-entity.rationale: Linking memories to entities mutates knowledge-graph provenance and needs review/undo semantics.
/api/memory/link-entity.next_action: Design a graph-authoring or entity-suggestion acceptance flow before exposing.

/api/memory/relations.rationale: Direct relation creation can corrupt the knowledge graph if exposed as a blind write.
/api/memory/relations.next_action: Design a relation editor/review flow with provenance and undo states.

/api/ping.rationale: The app uses typed /api/health and /api/status for health and status checks.

/api/steep.rationale: Manual steep/refinery work can invoke maintenance phases and should not be a raw desktop button.
/api/steep.next_action: Keep deferred; do not expose until a separate maintenance design covers cost, capability, progress, and failure states.

/ws/updates.rationale: The current app uses Tauri events, query invalidation, and status polling instead of daemon WebSocket subscriptions.
/ws/updates.next_action: Revisit only if replacing the app event flow with daemon events becomes an explicit product goal.
